definition: The historical base rate for commodity supercycle initiation is 2.4-3.1% annual probability, with an honest confidence interval of [0.4%, 8.2%]. Four real supercycles have been identified, delivering 200-400% real returns over 13-22 years each. The small sample (n=4) fundamentally limits statistical inference.
evidence: Four supercycles: 200-400% real returns over 13-22 years
n=4 limits all statistical inference including duration shortening claims
open_questions: Is the current period within the confidence interval for a new supercycle?
Does the wide confidence interval render the base rate practically useless?
